steps.title: Interpret zeros graphically
steps.content: Finding $f(x) = 0$ means locating where the curve intersects the x-axis. We count the x-intercepts.

steps.title: Count the x-intercepts from the graph
steps.content:
Scanning the cubic curve from left to right:
  • First intercept: near $x = -2$, the curve crosses from below to above the x-axis
  • Second intercept: near $x = 1$, the curve crosses from above to below the x-axis
  • Third intercept: near $x = 3$, the curve crosses from below to above the x-axis
The cubic function crosses the x-axis exactly three times. That matches choice C — C is correct.
